summarization.a: The chart shows the thickness of Al₂O₃ as a function of the number of cycles at two temperatures: a) 300°C and  b) 200°C. The thickness decreases with increasing cycles for both temperatures. In both cases the thicknesses measured by x-ray reflectivity (XRR) and spectroscopic ellipsometry (SE) agree with each other.

a.question: What are the initial thicknesses?
a.answer:
The initial Al2O3 films grown are
1. At 300 °C, it is 141 Å
2. At 200 °C, it is 170 Å

a.question: Does SE and XRR measurement yield similar results?
a.answer: The film thickness from XRR versus number of ALE cycles yields an etch rate of 0.46 Å/cycle. Thenspectroscopic ellipsometry (SE) measurements on these same samples also yield an etch rate of 0.46 Å/cycle. In addition, the etch rates determined by XRR and SE are similar to the etch rate obtained by the in situ QCM experiments at at 300 °C.

a.question: Why is the thickness from fitting of the data is higher than the initial observed thicknesses for 300 °C?
a.answer:
These slightly larger thicknesses originate from the mass gain that occurs during the nucleation
of the ALE process on the first ALE cycle.

a.question: Is there a linear dependence between the film thickness and number of ALE cycles ?
